mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate earnings over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return frequency above 55% over 60-day horizons in recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, triggering short-term equity sell-offs as investors anticipate margin compression.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative bias (p > 0.05) when industrial production falls during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profits and prompts expectations of restrictive monetary policy, generating negative forward equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 60% of months following inflation spikes above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive quarterly equity market performance.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.5) during periods of consistent payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and stagnant personal consumption expenditures signals weakening aggregate demand, leading to earnings downgrades and negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive returns in > 55% of cases over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above its long-term trend indicates accelerating manufacturing output and capacity utilization, triggering short-term equity rallies as investors price in improved corporate margins.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO exceeds its 12-month moving average by 1.5%
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns fail to show a positive bias (frequency < 52%) when the production threshold is breached.
